new_classes.definition_en: Sparkling wine of fresh grapes falling within CN subheading 2204 10 (sparkling wine), other than Champagne, Cava, Prosecco, and other named sparkling wines with specific subheadings; classified under CN 2204 10 94.

coverage_explanation: The legal text for CN 2204 10 94 covers sparkling wine (subheading note 1 to chapter 22 applies, defining sparkling wine by excess pressure ≥ 3 bar at 20 °C). The TBox already defines SparklingWine and named subclasses (Champagne, Prosecco). CN 2204 10 94 is the residual 'other' sparkling wine bucket within 2204 10 (i.e. not Champagne 22041011, not Cava, not Prosecco, and not other named varieties with their own 8-digit codes). The axioms correctly subclass SparklingWine and exclude Champagne and Prosecco production regions. However, the full set of named exclusions (Cava and any other named sparkling wines with dedicated subheadings between 22041011 and 22041093) cannot be fully captured without dedicated sibling class IRIs for those nodes in the TBox, reducing coverage. The excess-pressure threshold (≥ 3 bar at 20 °C from subheading note 1) is already modelled on the parent SparklingWine class via ExcessPressureQuality in the TBox, so it need not be repeated here.
